title: Kickoff — Stage 1 harvest harness underway
body:
Goal (from KICKOFF.md, the acceptance contract): build the Archon-orchestrated pipeline that turns the internet's free procedural Blender add-ons into a local, hash-verified, capability-tagged corpus — then run only SPEC §9 steps 1–3 and STOP at the mandatory decision gate.

  • Step 1 — repo + Archon init (3 workflows, 3 commands, scripts) → archon validate workflows clean.
  • Step 2 — sandbox + probe.py + golden set → 100% correct pass/fail/legacy/quarantine across Blender 3.6 / 4.2 / 4.5.
  • Step 3 — L1 thin slice (official extensions platform) over Terrain + Vegetation → real coverage numbers → reports/gate-decision.md.

Then I hand the go/no-go to you. Steps 4–8 stay untouched. A fresh-context adversarial reviewer (Claude Fable) re-verifies every bar item before I claim done.

Environment probed: archon ✓, uv 0.8.4 ✓, bun 1.3.14 ✓, gh 2.93.0 ✓. Docker client 28.3.2 present; daemon was down — I just launched Docker Desktop and am waiting for it (the golden set needs it).

title: Sandbox base image: SPEC assumption doesn't hold — pivoting to pinned official tarballs
body:
Reality check (working rule #5): SPEC §1 assumed linuxserver/blender ships "one tag per Blender version." Live Docker Hub query shows it only publishes the current release — every tag is 5.1.x / latest. No 3.6 / 4.2 / 4.5.

Plan: build the three-version matrix from official download.blender.org tarballs, each pinned by SHA-256, on a digest-pinned base image. This is more reproducible than a rolling image and gives exact 3.6 / 4.2 / 4.5. I will add a dated divergence note to SPEC §1 when I commit the sandbox, rather than silently diverging.

Note: this Mac is arm64 and Blender ships Linux as x86-64 only, so probe containers run --platform linux/amd64 under emulation. Fine for a ~15-artifact golden set of light headless operations.

title: OPEN QUESTION → need the taxonomy source (blocking for step 3 only)
body:
SPEC §0 requires taxonomy.yaml (~215 static + 72 animation niches across 12 categories), built from your two source documents. It is not in the repo, and I will not invent it (the PRD's zero-fabrication rule applies to me too).

This blocks only step 3 (the L1 thin-slice coverage over Terrain + Vegetation). Steps 1 (Archon init) and 2 (sandbox + golden set) do not need it, so I am proceeding on those now while I wait. Asking you how you want to hand over the taxonomy.

title: Kickoff inputs received — taxonomy v2 verified, 2 SPEC amendments recorded
body:
Taxonomy delivered as a committed file (inputs/taxonomy.yaml, v2). I hand-checked the meta counts and they are internally consistent:

  • declared_total 287 = 215 static + 72 animation ✓
  • wave-1 present 269 = 287 − 16 unrecovered − 2 crossref-dedup ✓
  • wave-2 present 59 · present_total 328 ✓
  • Step-3 gate denominator = Terrain (36) + Vegetation (23) wave-1 = 59 niches (wave-2 entries excluded from gate math)

Owner directives folded in: gate computes on wave-1 only; corrupted niches counted (not placeheld); parse-taxonomy → validate not parse; coverage adds reports/taxonomy-proposals.md; ids freeze at first coverage run.

Two dated SPEC amendments committed (working rule #5 — never diverge silently): §4.4 verb enum +aggregate and verbs:[] allowed; §1 sandbox pivots to SHA-256-pinned download.blender.org tarballs (owner-endorsed). Consolidated in new SPEC §12.1.

title: Step-1 evidence: taxonomy VALIDATES + L1 API mapped (1240 extensions, hashes included)
body:
Taxonomy validator PASSES (deterministic, validate_taxonomy.py) — every meta count is internally consistent and the identity check holds. This is the repurposed parse-taxonomy = validate step (§12.1(3)).

L1 API reverse-engineered live — GET https://extensions.blender.org/api/v1/extensions/ returns the whole catalog in one call: 1240 items = 998 add-ons + 242 themes. Each add-on ships archive_url and archive_hash (sha256), plus type, blender_version_min, license, tags, maintainer. So L1 acquire + provenance (bar g) is fully deterministic: download, confirm the hash equals the API-declared one.

Key find (the 4.2 migration in action): the bundled A.N.T. Landscape, Sapling Tree Gen, IvyGen are ALSO published as downloadable extensions on L1 — so they become real Terrain/Vegetation vault entries with live URLs + hashes, not just "bundled." Seed anchors confirmed on L1: Tissue, Bagapie, Modular Tree, Wiggle Bones, Wiggle 2, Camera Shakify, Scatter Objects. Not on L1 (GitHub/marketplace): Sverchok, Archipack, JewelCraft, BookGen, blender-osm, BlenderGIS, CrowdMaster, Rigacar, Botaniq, Buildify — verifying those now.

title: Seed anchors live-verified (21) + Blender matrix pinned by SHA-256
body:
inputs/seed-anchors.yaml written — all 21 anchors live-verified 2026-07-05 against fetched sources (extensions.blender.org API, GitHub manifests/raw source, Blender manual). Never from memory. 5 of 7 verification agents did real tool-backed work (18–41 web calls each); the 2 that returned instantly with 0 tool-calls were discarded and self-verified.

Confirmed reality that shapes the golden set: A.N.T. Landscape / Sapling / IvyGen were bundled in 3.6/4.1 but removed in the 4.2 migration — now L1 extensions (min ≥4.2). Several anchors are min 4.4/4.5 (correct pass = 4.5 only). This gives the golden set natural per-version state variety.

Sandbox pinned (SPEC §12.1(1)): building the 3-version matrix from official tarballs, each SHA-256-verified live:
code:
Blender 3.6.23  sha256 0e9a18af4d0060b825e9617e24a775f759e0f9f67271c062f3d53a539030af00
Blender 4.2.22  sha256 b0064f2879b269787375f6b9021fefa6ae478e1d16aca839f5c63ac36fb02fe3
Blender 4.5.11  sha256 05ed7bd41bf3e61ae4f4a7cdc364c43088bf8b3fed702c2269c018fdf63a2188

L1 Terrain+Vegetation thin slice: 15 candidates (from 893 unique add-ons)

title: ✅ Step 2 DONE — sandbox + probe + golden set: 100% correct across 3.6 / 4.2 / 4.5 (bar b)
body:
The Gate is calibrated. Three digest-pinned probe images (Blender 3.6.23 / 4.2.22 / 4.5.11, built from SHA-256-verified official tarballs, run under amd64 emulation). probe.py runs the SPEC §3.1 five-check ladder inside a --network none, read-only, non-root, cap-dropped container. The deterministic acceptance test tests/check_golden.py reports PASS — every golden state + prescan correct:

artifact3.64.24.5what it proves
golden_good_cubepasspasspassfull 5-check pass path
golden_nooppartialpartialpartialenables but no geometry
golden_legacy_27xlegacylegacylegacypre-2.8 API never enables
golden_brokenfailfailfailinvalid archive
antlandscapefailpartialpartialext min 4.2; dialog generator
ivygenfailpartialpartialext min 4.2
tissuefailpasspassext; operator drives headless
cellfracturefailpasspassext; fracture makes geometry
saplingfailfailpassversion gating (min 4.4)

Prescan gate: golden_danger → needs_review (subprocess/eval/__import__/urllib) ✓; benign fixtures → clean ✓. All 5 states exercised (quarantine via the 110s wall-clock cap). Honest finding: dialog-only generators (A.N.T. landscape_add returns PASS_THROUGH headless) correctly land at partial — which still counts toward coverage.

Render below = headless Cycles-CPU frame produced inside the sandbox (proves §3.1 step 5):

media.caption: Headless Cycles-CPU render inside the --network none sandbox (Blender 4.5)

title: ✅ Step 1 DONE — Archon harness authored + validates clean (bar a)
body:
archon validate workflows → all three exit 0: harvest-source, verify-batch, coverage-report. (The one error / one warning in the listing are Archon bundled defaults with missing optional MCP/skill files — not this project.)

Authored per SPEC §2.3 / §6: config.yaml (secrets via env only); 3 workflows; 3 commands (parse-taxonomy=validate, enrich-manifest, write-coverage); scripts — L1 enumerator (functional), L2–L5 honest stubs (steps 4–8 untouched), normalize/acquire/prescan/verify_matrix/build_index/coverage/validate_taxonomy. Determinism split honored: only enrich + report are AI nodes; the L5 acquire-gate is a human approval (interactive: true); harvest forces worktree isolation.

Now executing the L1 thin slice end-to-end (Terrain + Vegetation).

title: ✅ Step 3 DONE — L1 thin slice end-to-end; DECISION GATE reached (bars c, d, g)
body:
Full loop ran on the L1 extensions lane over Terrain + Vegetation: enumerate → normalize → acquire(+SHA-256) → prescan(+human clearance) → verify(3-version sandbox) → enrich → index → coverage. 11 add-ons vaulted, each with meta.json + a hash that matched the platform-declared SHA-256 (zero fabrication, bar g). corpus.db rebuilds idempotently (identical content hash across two runs, SPEC §8).

PRD §4 metricresultstop-line
Coverage — Terrain+Veg wave-17 / 59 = 11.9%<40%line is post-L2; L1-only floor
Acquisition pass-rate10 / 11 = 90.9%<30%3× above — ecosystem is healthy

Covered wave-1 niches (7): terrain_generator, heightmap_stack_tools, erosion_sim, snow_accumulation, tree_generator, space_colonization_growth, ivy_generator.

Recommendation → PROCEED to L2 (premise survives; nothing trips a stop-line). reports/gate-decision.md has the full reasoning. The go/no-go is yours. Honest caveats logged: bagapie quarantine under amd64 emulation (≠ fail); prescan flagged 4/11 (all human-cleared after source inspection, 1 pure false positive); the 4.2 extension migration means all extensions fail on 3.6.

Steps 4–8 untouched. Next: adversarial fresh-context review (Claude Fable) re-verifies every bar item before I claim done.

title: 🔒 REVIEW GATE: PASS — adversarial fresh-context reviewer (Claude Fable 5) re-verified all bars a–g
body:
A fresh-context, read-only, adversarial reviewer (pinned claude-fable-5, def in .claude/agents/reviewer.md) was given ONLY KICKOFF/PRD/SPEC + the repo — no summary from me. It independently re-ran every check (62 tool calls, ~7 min):

barhow it was independently reproduced
a · validatorPASSran archon validate workflows ×3 → all exit 0
b · golden setPASSdeleted reports, re-ran check_golden.py --run → VERDICT PASS; images present
c · thin slicePASS11 vault sha256 ✓; rebuilt corpus.db twice → identical content hash; coverage 7/59 reproduced
d · gate disciplinePASSgate-decision.md exists; only L1 candidates; L2–L5 confirmed honest stubs by source
e · securityPASSzero secret hits in history+tree; vault/db git-ignored (verified); sandbox flags + prescan-before-container confirmed
f · progress pagePASSserver live (HTTP 200); timestamped entries; render PNG is real 480×270 image
g · zero fabricationPASS5/5 random vault entries re-downloaded + re-hashed = exact match; cross-checked against a fresh live API pull

VERDICT: PASS — no reliance on the builder's claims, no secrets, no scope creep past step 3, no idempotency drift, no fabricated/dead-linked entries.

Honest correction the reviewer surfaced: progress entry #6 noted "15 candidates" — that was a mid-tuning figure. After I tightened the enumerator stoplist (dropping 4 false positives: bonsai/PlaceHelper/import_mixamo/script_launcher), the shipped slice is 11 candidates, which the reviewer confirmed is byte-reproducible from the live API right now. Not a bar failure; recording it for an accurate log.

GATE DECISION handed to the owner (yours to make): coverage 7/59 = 11.9% (Terrain+Veg wave-1; the <40% line is formally post-L2), acquisition pass-rate 10/11 = 90.9% (PRD stop-line <30%). Nothing trips a stop condition. Recommendation → PROCEED to L2. Full reasoning in reports/gate-decision.md. Steps 4–8 remain untouched pending your go/no-go.

title: L2 GitHub yield spike (SPEC §10) — token validated, ecosystem is large
body:
GH_TOKEN validated (rider 2): gh api rate_limit auth OK as notaustindaniels (core 5000, search 30/min, code_search 10/min). It is a fine-grained token (empty scopes) — the case you flagged as sometimes rejected by code search — so I tested an actual code-search query: blender_manifest.toml in:path → 1,512. Code search works; no fallback token needed. Token read from env only, never echoed/logged/committed; .archon/.env confirmed git-ignored.

Yield per signature (authenticated, reports/l2-yield.json):

kindsignaturetotal_count
codemanifest extensions — blender_manifest.toml1,512
codelegacy add-ons — bl_info in Python35,072
codelegacy (tight) — "bl_info = {"24,640
repotopic:blender-addon1,812
repotopic:blender-plugin161
repotopic:blender-extension47
repotopic:geometry-nodes57
repoTerrain-scoped (topic:blender-addon terrain)6
repoVegetation-scoped (topic:blender-addon tree)107

Implication (rider 5): raw signature counts are in the tens of thousands (inflated by forks / non-addon files / archived repos). Even the clean topic universe is ~1,800 repos. This is well past "hundreds," so a native amd64 probe path is now a decision for the owner — I will scope the exact Terrain+Vegetation acquirable subset first, then bring you that decision with a concrete count. Meanwhile I am building the L2 infra: version-aware matrix, timeout→retry state, prescan-throughput allowlist, pass/partial coverage split.

title: D-001 ACKNOWLEDGED — riders R1–R11 mapped to their committed encodings
body:
Per the DECISIONS.md durability protocol (a rider counts only when its encoding is committed), each rider is now mapped to its durable encoding. Governance obligation added: CLAUDE.md now mandates re-reading KICKOFF.md, SPEC §12, and DECISIONS.md at session start + after compaction.

DECISIONS.md D-001 = 6d75b4d. Proceeding: L2 enumeration (R11, in flight) → acquire (star-ordered, bounded) → gate → version-aware verify → coverage → formal PRD §4 re-evaluation.

title: L2 complete (bounded) — PRD §4 re-evaluation: premise holds, coverage not yet decision-grade
body:
L2 GitHub lane ran end-to-end (spike → enumerate → acquire → gate → version-aware verify → coverage). R11 enumeration recorded 27 active T+V candidates + 136 graveyard records from 160 repos (archived / no-signature / legacy-2.7x — recorded, never silently skipped). Top-15 by stars acquired + gated + verified.

metricL1L1+L2stop-line
Coverage — Terrain+Veg wave-111.9%7/59 = 11.9% (4 full-pass + 3 partial)<40%
Acquisition pass-rate90.9%13/21 = 61.9%<30%

Honest L2 findings: GitHub survival ~30% (3 of 10 non-gated) vs L1's 91% — the ecosystem-rot thesis, quantified. The danger gate correctly blocked 5 (eval/exec/os.system/ctypes/base64 — incl. blender-python-nodes, a code-executor by design). L2 upgraded erosion_sim to full-pass (petak5/bp) but added no new niche — its survivors mapped to niches L1 already covered.

Formal PRD §4 reading: coverage 11.9% < 40% is literally crossed — BUT L2 here was a bounded 15-of-27 sample (native-probe decision unresolved), and the 59-niche denominator is dominated by exotic niches (karst, coral_atoll, gas_giant) with little free tooling, while common niches with real add-ons (dune, river, flower, grass…) were simply outside the bounded harvest. So 11.9% is a lower bound, not decision-grade. The premise metric (pass-rate) stays healthy.

Recommendation → the premise decision the PRD designed the gate for (owner's call, next DECISIONS entry): approve the native amd64 probe path to mass-verify the L2 long tail and make the coverage real, then re-evaluate; and/or recalibrate the provisional 40% target. Steps 6–8 untouched (R9). Full reasoning in reports/prd4-reeval.md.

title: L2 review cycle 1: FAIL → fixed (2 commit-hygiene defects; engineering sound)
body:
Fresh-context adversarial reviewer (Claude Fable 5, 91 tool calls, incl. an empirical NEVER_ALLOW bypass attempt) independently PASSED items 2–8: coverage math (7/59=11.9%, 13/21=61.9%, idempotent), version-aware/timeout mechanics, the danger gate (5 L2 blocked, un-bypassable), R11 graveyard (136 records), provenance (3 immutable-tag L2 hashes re-downloaded + matched), token hygiene (no leak in history/reports/feed), and scope (steps 6–8 untouched).

It correctly FAILED two commit-hygiene defects (not security/integrity): (1) committed prescan-findings showed a stale FP rate 18.2%/9-blocked — re-running reproduces 54.5%/5-blocked (predated the 4 L2 clearances); (2) the D-001 acknowledgment feed entry + prd4-reeval.md provenance note lived only in the uncommitted working tree, so per the durability protocol they didn't yet "count". Both fixed: findings regenerated, SPEC stale path (reviews→policies) corrected, and all of it — including this feed — is being committed now. Re-verification requested.

title: 🔒 L2 REVIEW GATE: PASS (cycle 2) — full adversarial review clean at c477ef4
body:
Review cycle 2 re-verified the two cycle-1 defects from the committed tree and both now PASS: (1a) reports/prescan-findings.json committed with FP-rate 54.5% / 5 still-blocked, reproduces byte-for-byte with zero drift; (1b) progress/feed.json committed with all 14 entries incl. the D-001 acknowledgment (seq 12), and prd4-reeval.md provenance note committed; (nit) SPEC §12.2(3) path corrected to policies/.

Combined with cycle-1's independently-reproduced items 2–8 — coverage arithmetic (7/59=11.9%, 13/21=61.9%, idempotent), version-aware/timeout mechanics, the danger gate holding under an empirical NEVER_ALLOW bypass attempt, R11 ordering/graveyard exactness (136 records), 3/3 provenance hash matches, a clean token-hygiene sweep (incl. dangling git objects), and scope discipline — VERDICT: PASS. The L2 phase of Stage 1→2 is durably sound.

Handing the premise decision to the owner (next DECISIONS.md entry). Steps 6–8 remain untouched per R9. Recommendation: approve the native amd64 probe path to mass-verify the L2 long tail (the 11.9% is a bounded-sample lower bound, not decision-grade) and/or recalibrate the provisional 40% target — the premise is not falsified (pass-rate 61.9%).

title: D-002 ACKNOWLEDGED — repair the instrument, then re-evaluate (R12–R17)
body:
Read D-002 (canonical). The PRD §4 stop-line is honored: harvest scaling stays paused (no L3/L4/L5); the convened decision is repair the coverage instrument, then re-run the same evaluation. Option 4 (L3/L4 now) rejected; option 3 (paid-vs-build) deferred to D-003. Acknowledging R12–R17 per protocol:

riderencoding targetstatus
R12 review the blocked 5 firstR6 batched review + allowlist updatestarting now
R13 native amd64 (needs sign-off)proposal file + feed; SPEC §12 after approvalproposal next — then I WAIT for your reply
R14 recipes first-classcode (coverage + registry schema) + SPEC §12; recipe_verified vs recipe_unverifiedrule committed 5bdd63b; build pending
R15 calibration memo (no fiat)reports/coverage-calibration.md — per-niche market audit of the 59pending
R16 formal re-eval on decision-grade datareports/prd4-reeval-2.md + D-003 request; pass-rate of-probed AND of-all-acquisitionspending (needs R13 native matrix)
R17 stop-lines pause scalingCLAUDE.md standing rulecommitted 5bdd63b

Sequence: R12 review → R13 proposal (await reply) → R14 build + R15 memo (parallel, no native needed) → native re-probe on approval → R16 re-eval → D-003 request. Steps 6–8 untouched throughout.

title: R12 done + R13 proposal posted (awaiting sign-off) — recovery is blocked by the emulation defect
body:
R12 — reviewed the 5 prescan-blocked L2 at source. Cleared 2 (sorcar: opt-in script node, numeric eval, updater urllib — none fire during verification; trailprint3d: exec/import were test-only). Kept 3 blocked with findings recorded (reports/prescan-blocked-findings.md): blender-python-nodes (confirmed arbitrary-Python executor), witcher3-tools, fastpbr. Instrument repair: prescan now skips test files + full-line comments (removed 2 context FPs; golden_danger still gates). FP-rate metric = 72.7% (8/11 flags benign — the scanner is aggressive, honestly tracked).

But the recovery is blocked by the very defect R13 fixes: re-probed, sorcar fails to enable on all 3 versions under emulation (a large node system — the same class of emulation failure as bagapie), and trailprint3d declares min Blender 5.1 → skipped on our matrix. 0 coverage recovered on the emulated matrix. Coverage holds at 7/59 = 11.9%; pass-rate 59.1% of-probed / 50.0% of-all-acquisitions (both framings now reported, per R16/D-002).

R13 — native amd64 probe proposal posted (reports/native-probe-proposal.md): GitHub Actions on this public repo at $0 preferred (native amd64, no emulation, sandbox flags intact, ephemeral); fallback a ≤$25/mo ephemeral cloud VM. Awaiting your sign-off — I will not provision or build until you approve. Its approval unblocks the full 27-candidate native L2 matrix + the L1 emulation-suspects → decision-grade coverage → R16.

title: R13 workflow BUILT — but push blocked: token lacks write to blender-engine (needs your action)
body:
Option A workflow built + committed (.github/workflows/native-probe.yml, commit dfb7af6). My local HEAD is a clean fast-forward — 5 commits ahead of your D-002 (da72b8b) on public blender-engine, no divergence.

Blocker (reported plainly, not worked around — R2): the provided GH_TOKEN authenticates as notaustindaniels but returns 403 (write denied) on push — it is a read/enumeration-scoped fine-grained token without Contents:write / Workflows:write on this repo, and no SSH key is configured. So I cannot push the workflow, and GHA cannot run it until it is on the repo.

Your options to unblock the native run: (a) push my 5 commits yourself (clean fast-forward: git pull then git push from your credentialed clone), or (b) provide a token with Contents:write + Workflows:write on blender-engine and I will push + trigger it. Meanwhile I am NOT idle — proceeding with R14 (recipes as first-class coverage) and R15 (per-niche calibration memo), which do not need the native run.

title: R14 (recipes first-class) + R15 (calibration memo) done — 90% of the 59 niches are free-attainable
body:
R14 — recipes are first-class coverage. inputs/recipes.yaml registry (niche → composition of vaulted operators / built-ins); coverage.py counts recipe_verified in the decision % but shows recipe_unverified as a SEPARATE claimed tier (never inflating the gate). 5 seeded recipes, all unverified (no recipe-probe has run — honest). Gate holds at 7/59 = 11.9% (4 full-pass + 3 partial + 0 recipe-verified) + 5 claims shown.

R15 — market-existence audit of all 59 Terrain+Veg wave-1 niches (I ran this myself with live web research after the parallel agents hit an auth blip; cached catalog + WebSearch/WebFetch, evidence + confidence per niche in reports/coverage-calibration-data.yaml):

verdictnichesshare
verified_free (already probed)814%
free_tool (exists, not yet vaulted)1017%
free_recipe (built-ins/GN/free packs)3559%
paid_only35%
none35%
ATTAINABLE (free path exists)5390%

Finding: ~90% of these niches have some free path (GitHub / free GN pack / Gumroad-$0 / built-in recipe) — they just don't live on the tiny official platform, and the emulation-capped L2 sample barely touched them. Only 6 are genuinely not-free (coral/kelp/anemone are paid; karst/coral_atoll/meander have no market). The 40% target isn't unreachable — our metric undercounts by counting only probe-verified dedicated add-ons. Memo proposes (for D-003, no fiat change): measure vs the attainable base, and/or complete the harvest (native probe + recipes + GN-pack sources). reports/coverage-calibration.md.

Remaining before R16: the native run (R13) — blocked on the push/permission you need to unblock. R16 formal re-eval + the D-003 request follow once decision-grade native data exists.

title: R13 native run: pushed ✓ but dispatch blocked — token missing Actions:write
body:
R13 progress: reconciled with origin ✓, validated GH_TOKEN_RW (push=true) ✓, R15 addendum done (URLs on all 15 load-bearing rows; meander→free_recipe; attainable 54/59=92%) ✓, pushed 0b4bad8..6e1de4a via RW token ✓ (never printed).

Blocker (R2 — reported, not worked around): workflow_dispatch needs Actions:write; the RW token has Actions:read + Contents:write but NOT Actions:write (dispatch → 403). No push-trigger hack applied.

Two clean fixes: (a) add Actions: Read and write to the fine-grained token permissions and I retry the dispatch immediately; or (b) trigger it yourself: GitHub → Actions → native-probe → Run workflow (main). Either way I can then watch the run + download artifacts (Actions:read already works).

title: R13 dispatch retry: still 403 (fine-grained PAT can't workflow_dispatch) — 3 clean paths, your pick
body:
Re-ran the dispatch after your re-sent unblock. Reconciled with origin first (in sync at 6e1de4a). The RW token still returns 403 on workflow_dispatch — same precise gap as before: it has Actions:read + Contents:write (push works) but the dispatch endpoint is denied. This is a known GitHub limitation: fine-grained PATs frequently cannot trigger workflow_dispatch even when "Actions: read and write" is set. Per R2 I am reporting this, not working around it.

Three clean paths — your call:

  • (a) Run it yourself (fastest, zero risk): GitHub → Actions → native-probe → Run workflow → branch main. I already have Actions:read, so I will poll the run and download the artifact the moment it appears — no token change needed.
  • (b) Switch token type: a classic PAT with the workflow scope can dispatch (the fine-grained one reliably can't). Drop it in as GH_TOKEN_RW and I retry immediately.
  • (c) Add a gated push: trigger to the workflow so my push (Contents:write, which works) starts it — but this bypasses the dispatch-permission gate, so it needs your explicit OK (it brushes the R13 "never worked around" guardrail; I will not do it unilaterally).

All the pre-run work is done and pushed: R15 addendum (URLs on all 15 load-bearing verdicts, meander re-searched → 54/59=92% attainable), native-probe.yml on origin, both tokens validated. Only the trigger is blocked. Recommend (a) — one click and I take it from there through R16 + the D-003 request.

title: R13 native run DISPATCHED — run 28825791382 (queued) on native amd64
body: Token fixed (Actions: Read+write), re-validated, checked for in-flight runs (none), and dispatched the native-probe workflow once → GitHub Actions run 28825791382 (queued) on ubuntu-latest (native amd64, no emulation). Scope: all 27 L2 candidates + L1 emulation-suspects (bagapie ×2, terrain-mixer@4.5) + the 2 R12-cleared (sorcar, trailprint3d). Polling for completion; on finish I pull the artifact, index, promote recipes to recipe_verified only where a probe ran, then write R16 + the D-003 request.

title: Hook repair (item 3): no persistent hook to patch — session-scoped, already cleared; standing rule recorded instead
body:
Investigated the authorized hook repair. The goal-keeper Stop hook was session-scoped — registered dynamically by the /goal harness command, NOT stored in any settings file — and /goal clear already removed it. Confirmed: neither ~/.claude/settings.json nor settings.local.json contains a hooks block; no persistent goal condition remains on disk (only a clipboard paste-cache).

The honest "diff": no settings patch is warranted. I will not add a hooks block to your global config on the (now-disproven) premise that one exists there — that would be risky overreach. If the loop-tolerant hook you want lives somewhere I have not found (e.g. a harness-managed template), point me at the exact file and I will post a real diff before touching it.

Durable deliverable applied: the pattern is now a CLAUDE.md standing rule — "Awaiting-owner is a valid terminal state; never loop against it." (commit e5711f2). Meanwhile the native run 28825791382 is executing; polling it now.

title: ✅ R13 native run complete + R16 re-eval — decision-grade 13.6%; D-003 request handed to owner
body:
Native amd64 run 28825791382 succeeded (ubuntu-latest, no emulation): all 27 L2 candidates acquired + probed (vs the bounded 15 emulated), golden set re-passed natively. Ingested native manifests (they supersede emulated — 11 state changes).

PRD §4 metricemulated (15)native (all 27)line
Coverage — T+V wave-17/59 = 11.9%8/59 = 13.6% (5 full + 3 partial + 0 recipe✓)<40%
Pass-rate of-probed59.1%15/27 = 55.6%<30%
Pass-rate of-all-acquisitions50.0%15/37 = 40.5%<30%

Native surfaced: beneking102/bene-proggen-maps (full pass → terrain_generator now full-pass) and ra100/planet-gen (partial → new niche alien_biome_generator); petak5/bp full-pass on all 3. Honest corrections: bagapie quarantines even natively (real crash, not emulation); the L2 tail is genuinely rotten (12/27 fail/legacy/blocked). Recipes stay unverified — the 5 seeded ones compose dialog-gated operators (still PASS_THROUGH natively), so 0 moved the gate (rider 4 honored).

Formal reading: 13.6% < 40% is crossed on decision-grade data — but the R15 memo (now URL-sourced) shows 54/59 = 92% of these niches have a free path; the gap is unbuilt recipe-verification + unharvested GN-pack/marketplace sources, not ecosystem poverty. Pass-rate (premise metric) is healthy. Premise not falsified.

D-003 decision request handed to you (reports/prd4-reeval-2.md): (1) build recipe-verification + measure vs the attainable base [recommended]; (2) open GN-pack/marketplace + BlenderKit lanes where the missing free tools live; (3) convene paid-vs-build [only if 1&2 declined]. Steps 6–8 stay frozen until your D-003 entry (R17). The decision is yours.

Proceeding to L3 → L4; L5 returns to you for approval batches (R20). Steps stay gated at each lane by the R19 tripwire.

title: L3 + L4 link-routers done — tripwire OK; L5 now needs your approval batches (84 links queued)
body:
L3 (BlenderArtists forum) + L4 (BlenderNation) link-routers built and run (R20; both automated, no browser, per SPEC §2.2).

  • L3 — Discourse .json on the live-discovered Released-Scripts category (coding/50): 90 topics → 14 new GitHub repos + 84 L5-pending marketplace links + 1 dead-link → graveyard.
  • L4 — BlenderNation wp-json is WAF-blocked (403), so switched to RSS feeds (exactly SPEC §2.2's "RSS/archive scrape"; §12.4 records the pivot). Parses posts; 0 tool-links in the current RSS window — honest zero (deep archive is WAF-gated).

R19 tripwire at the L3/L4 lane gate: pass-rate of-all-acquisitions 40.5% > 30% floor → OK, clear to proceed. Gate v2 unchanged at 5/54 = 9.3% — L3's 14 repos are retopo/UV/lighting utilities, 0 new T+V coverage. This is the same head pile-up L2 showed, and it confirms D-003's basis: the path to 40% runs through L5 GN-packs + recipe verification, not more GitHub-style lanes.

⏭ L5 is next and it returns to YOU (R20): L5 acquisition is human-gated — 84 marketplace links (Gumroad / Superhive / Fab, from L3) are queued in candidates/L5_pending.jsonl. Per the guardrails: ToS read first, GitHub-mirror rerouting before any checkout, approval-node $0-checkout batches you complete, receipts creator-visible. I will not run L5 acquisition without your approval. Want me to (a) build the L5 discovery/dedup + ToS-check pass and prepare the first approval batch, or (b) prioritize the recipe-drive + GN-pack angle first?

title: L5/A-lane ToS-check pass (FIRST, before any batch) — material finding: Gumroad forbids scraping
body:
ToS read live before preparing any batch (your build order). Findings in policies/marketplace-tos.md (commit 9e42e05):

sourceToS findingposture
L5a Gumroad§14 explicitly forbids "spiders, robots, scrapers, crawlers… to scrape or download data from any web pages." Buyer downloading own library not restricted; assisted $0 checkout not addressed.NO automated scraping — fully human-gated discovery+checkout; mirror-check via web search, not page scraping. (Stricter than SPEC §5.4 assumed — §12.5 amendment.)
L5b SuperhiveToS page is WAF-blocked (403) — UNREAD by me. Free membership; per-product license = buyer responsibility.Fully human-gated + FLAG: needs a manual ToS read by you before any L5b automation. Runs last.
A1 SketchfabOfficial Download API for 700k+ CC models; OAuth token; must display CC license + attribution + "provided by Sketchfab".API-automatable for CC content, gated on SKETCHFAB_TOKEN. No scraping, no checkout — ToS-blessed.
A2 ArtStation / A3 FabdeferredToS pass deferred to activation (R29) — only after L5a batch 1; posted here before any automation.

Consequence for the L5 build: I will NOT run automated Playwright scraping on Gumroad. The 84 L5-pending links were already discovered legitimately via the L3 forum; I will (1) mirror-check each via web search (reroute GitHub-hosted ones to L2/L3, free + automatable), (2) rank the remainder per R11 by GN-pack likelihood + empty verb×medium grid cells + uncovered attainable niches, and (3) prepare the first ~20-row human approval batch in your 5-second format. Encoding R25–R29 + the recipe pivot next; then the batch. Two asks: a manual Superhive ToS read, and (when ready) the Sketchfab account + SKETCHFAB_TOKEN for A1.

title: D-004 ACKNOWLEDGED (R25–R29) + L5 approval batch #1 ready — recipe pivot moved gate v2 to 11.1%
body:
D-004 acknowledged (R25–R29 → commits): R25 A-lane semantics off-gate + R26 license load-bearing + R27 asset probe (probe_asset.py) + R28 ToS posture → SPEC §12.5 / policies/marketplace-tos.md (9e42e05, 62bef3d); R29 priority → CLAUDE.md.

Recipe pivot (R21/D-004) delivered a real gate movement: recomposed orchard_row_scatter = sapling tree_add (non-modal) + ARRAY builtin → recipe_verified (first machine-checked recipe, via the R21 probe not assertion). 4 modal-only recipes honestly parked reason=modal. Gate v2: 5/54 → 6/54 = 11.1%.

⏭ L5 approval batch #1 ready for you (reports/l5-batch-1.md, commit 705f380) — 10 rows, R11-ranked to move gate v2 + fill empty air/water grid cells, all UNCOVERED attainable niches:

  • 4 GitHub auto-route (no checkout, no ToS issue — I acquire on your go): asteroid, planetary_ring, cliff_rockface, crater_field.
  • 6 human-gated $0 (your 5-sec checkout): river (CGMatter Gumroad $0), waterfall (Blender Studio), fern (80.lv), flower + grass + ground_cover (Graswald/BD3D free).

Each row: target niche · medium/verbs · product · source · mirror-check→route · $0-assertion · license. Approve → I auto-acquire the 4 GitHub rows (hash-verified) + probe them; you $0-checkout the 6; then all go through the standard probe (R21 for GN-packs).

Two asks still open: (1) a manual Superhive ToS read (WAF-blocked to me); (2) the Sketchfab account + SKETCHFAB_TOKEN when ready, to start A1 in parallel (R28/R29). A2/A3 stay deferred until after this batch ships (R29).

title: Batch #1 resolved + A1 Sketchfab live — gate v2 → 13.0%; fern is the one clean /bin/zsh checkout
body:
Batch #1 GitHub reroutes done: cliff_rockface_generator FULL PASS (GN-pack .blend — fixed probe.py to route zip-wrapped .blend packs to the gn_pack path), asteroid_generator partial, planetary_ring/crater fail (project scripts). Gate v2: 9.3% → 11.1% (orchard recipe) → 13.0% (cliff GN-pack) = 7/54.

Resolution pass (your binding condition) — honest, nothing assumed through:

  • ✅ fern → sagado.gumroad.com/l/ozxrlu — CONFIRMED $0 (PWYW, no restrictions). The one clean checkout for you.
  • ⚠️ river (CGMatter) — FLAGGED: $0 unconfirmable (mixes free + paid; a full paid course exists). Won't assume.
  • ❌ waterfall — DROPPED: Blender Studio $17/mo subscription (your flag confirmed).
  • ↪️ flower (Plant-Library) — REROUTE→A-lane: free commercial-OK asset pack, not a tool; feeds a recipe.
  • ↪️ grass, ground_cover (Graswald) — REROUTE→A-lane, NC-licensed → segregated (R26); free scatter is Biome-Reader.

Superhive ToS discharged by your advisor (no anti-scrape clause, but human-checkout-only) — recorded with honesty caveat. A1 Sketchfab live: token validated, discovery working; marine-trio pull = 24 CC assets (21 cc-by usable, 2 by-nc segregated), license+attribution captured per R26, reported as assets never coverage (R25).

Your move: (1) the fern $0 checkout (one link, ready); (2) confirm/deny river; (3) go-ahead for the marine-flora asset-fed recipe experiment (CC-BY coral/kelp + free scatter → R21 probe → could convert the paid-only trio to recipe-attainable). A2/A3 stay deferred until batch #1 ships (R29). Commit a3df5e8.

title: D-005 ACKNOWLEDGED (R30–R34) — walk-away contract; running all lanes to exhaustion now
body:
D-005 (walk-away contract) read + acknowledged. Involvement is now batch-driven: I run every authorized lane to exhaustion, queue owner-gated items in OWNER-QUEUE.md, and surface ONE consolidated R34 session (drained queue + prd4-final.md + D-00x). Encodings: R30/R31/R33 → CLAUDE.md; R32 → SPEC §12.6; queue created.

Dispositions (R31): fern APPROVED+executed by you (queued: deliver the .blend for probing); river DENIED (unconfirmable price = no); marine-flora recipe experiment APPROVED (CC-BY only, by-nc segregated).

Run plan (to exhaustion, feed-only until R34): marine asset-fed recipe experiment → broaden the free GN-pack/tool harvest for uncovered attainable niches → A2/A3 ToS reads (drop if forbidding) → L5b Superhive discovery + queue → recipe drive → taxonomy proposals → final v2 evaluation. Gate v2 currently 7/54 = 13.0%. Next owner contact = the R34 session.

title: 🏁 R34 SESSION READY — gate v2 9.3% → 29.8% (all machine-verified); premise NOT falsified
body:
Walk-away run complete (D-005). Worked every automatable lane to exhaustion; final v2 evaluation ready (R30 surface trigger). Progression 9.3% → 11.1% → 13.0% → 14.5% → 17.5% → 29.8%, every step machine-verified in the sandbox — zero asserted coverage.

Marine experiment (R25) fully succeeded: the entire kelp/coral/anemone trio — all previously PAID-ONLY — converted to recipe_verified from free CC-BY Sketchfab assets + composition. Same template converted grass/flower/mushroom/moss/succulent/bioluminescent/scree. A2 ArtStation + A3 Fab DROPPED (no automatable CC path). Automatable T+V ecosystem exhausted at 29.8%.

Honesty held: stopped the asset-fed drive at the point of semantic fit — the remaining niches are procedural generators (need GN-packs) or shaders (need a shader-probe); force-fitting would be fabrication. recipe_verified is tier-separated from full_pass everywhere.

Premise: NOT falsified (pass-rate clears both ways). Path to 40% needs no spend (shader-probe + a few $0 GN-pack checkouts). Your R34 session: reports/prd4-final.md + drained OWNER-QUEUE.md — fern file drop, premise signature, D-006 disposition. That is the only thing that needs you now.

title: D-006 acknowledged — R35 review FULL PASS, premise CONFIRMED (R36 signed); R37 begins
body:
D-006 acknowledgment — R35–R40 mapped to committed encodings. The walk-away run finally got the adversarial review D-005 skipped, and passed it.

  • R35 — adversarial review (KICKOFF item 7): fresh-context claude-fable-5 reviewer, artifacts only, bias-to-FAIL. OVERALL: FULL PASS on all five after 2 fix cycles (within the max-2 budget): (a) coverage 17/57=29.8% rebuilt byte-identical; (b) 4 random recipe_verified re-probed in-sandbox, flags confirmed live via docker inspect; (c) marine-trio CC-BY-4.0 + SHA-256 verified, by-nc segregated/unused; (d) 54→57 reconciled; (e) A2/A3 drops carry dated ToS findings, zero leakage. The two FAILs were both item (d) durability — amendment text correct but first uncommitted; now committed.
  • R36 — the signature: premise CONFIRMED, not merely unfalsified (pass-rate 56.2%/42.9% clears 30% both ways; marine-trio = affirmative evidence). Signed block appended to reports/prd4-final.md §D-006 R36; 40% line reclassified kill-criterion → execution milestone (SPEC §12.8); R23 auto-trigger armed (fires only if no-spend path completes still <40%).
  • R37 — no-spend completion: AUTHORIZED, starting now — fern .blend asset-probe first, then shader-probe variant, GN-pack $0 batch prep, recipe drive (incl. asteroid partial→recipe). All under existing guardrails.
  • R38 — unattainables parked: karst_formation + coral_atoll_generator → Stage-2 build-from-scratch backlog (no purchase). Encoding pending in this drive.
  • R39 — quality tiers: per-operator/per-recipe quality field (full generator vs quality:minimal asset-fed) into the handoff contract alongside the verb×medium grid. Encoding pending.
  • R40 — wave-3 intake: rock_generator_addon into the taxonomy-proposal flow (never auto-added; batches to the consolidated session). Logged in reports/taxonomy-proposals.md.

Durable encodings this milestone: SPEC §12.7 (ec2b740, marine 54→57), SPEC §12.8 (40% reclassified), reports/prd4-final.md §D-006 R36 signature. Next owner surface = the consolidated session (GN-pack $0 checkout batch + wave-3 proposals + any auto-triggered R23 request) — nothing interrupts before it.

title: Consolidated session — gate 31.6%, shader-probe built; 2 no-spend routes to 40%

R37 drive + consolidated owner session (D-006 R34). Premise CONFIRMED (R36); gate v2 29.8% → 31.6% (18/57).

  • fern_generator FULL PASS — your $0 PWYW GN-pack, probed sandboxed (488 eval verts, renders). Fixed probe_asset.py to count depsgraph-evaluated geometry so GN generators aren't mis-failed.
  • Shader-probe built + self-validated (R37a; delta 0.137≥0.02). Unlocks 5 free_recipe shader niches (cracked_earth/gas_giant/ice/organic_cell/snow_ice) the geometry/asset probes read as empty.
  • R38 karst+coral_atoll → Stage-2 backlog (no purchase). R39 quality tiers in the handoff contract (7 full_generator / 1 composed / 10 asset_fed_minimal). R40 rock_generator_addon → wave-2 rock_boulder_generator proposal (you decide).
  • Path to 40% = 5 niches. Route A: acquire+probe free shaders (agent-doable, no checkout). Route B: $0 GN-pack owner-checkout batch (targets grounded; resolution pass pending your go — no fabricated URLs). R23 priced-options: armed, NOT fired — two no-spend routes still open.

Full session: reports/owner-consolidated-2026-07-07.md; queue: OWNER-QUEUE.md. Awaiting owner: pick Route A / Route B / rule on wave-3.

title: 🔒 R35 INDEPENDENT REVIEW: PASS — premise CONFIRMED; parallel-work collision found + repaired
body:
My fresh-context adversarial review of the walk-away run: VERDICT PASS on all 5 R35 items + secrets, each independently reproduced (artifacts only, not narrative):

  • (a) coverage rebuilt → 17/57 = 29.8% exact (walk-away state) ✓
  • (b) 4 random recipe_verified re-probed in the sandbox → all reproduced geometry delta + render ✓
  • (c) marine trio provenance: all CC-BY, author + "provided by Sketchfab" attribution + sha256; no by-nc in any recipe ✓
  • (d) 54→57 denominator reconciled in SPEC §12.7, justified by artifacts ✓
  • (e) A2/A3 dropped with ToS findings; A1 working ✓ · no leaked secrets ✓
